_comment: Participant story: DJ Brendan. Layout stays in stories/brendan.html; this file holds editable copy only.

meta.title: Meet DJ Brendan — Who's to Say Foundation
meta.description: Brendan Bukowski found his loudest voice in music. Meet DJ Brendan and the Remix Studio at Who's to Say Foundation — and help fund the next set.

hero.eyebrow: A Who's to Say ? story · Vol. 02
hero.titleHtml:
Meet DJ Brendan.
Where Broadway beats meet unbound potential.
hero.lead: Brendan Bukowski found his loudest voice in music. In our Remix Studio he navigates his own playlists, practices his transitions, and goes on air as DJ Brendan — every beat a step toward more confidence and connection.
hero.portraitAlt: Brendan at the radio mixing board, headphones around his neck, under a DJ Brendan neon sign and an ON AIR light

meet.title: He built his own Top 5 musicals, Broadway Mix.
paragraphs: Brendan has a keen ear for classic rock and a heart full of Broadway show tunes. So he made a playlist that's entirely his — "DJ Brendan's Broadway Mix" — and he navigates it with real independence, cueing the next number and practicing the introductions between every song.
For Brendan, music isn't just entertainment. It's a way to engage confidently in a dialogue about something he is passionate about.

editorial1.titleHtml: From fan to DJ: mastering the mix.
editorial1.imageAlt: Brendan singing into a microphone along with a music video and on-screen lyrics
paragraphs: Step into Brendan's world and you'll find him creating his playlist with real independence — navigating Spotify, setting up collaborative shares, and practicing smooth transitions between his favorite Broadway anthems. He's not just listening. He's hosting and sharing, because he believes music is for everyone.
With personalized headphone coaching, he's honing the craft of a radio show host one song intro at a time.
And the music is doing something bigger: his communication is flourishing — from simple responses to joyful, sustained, lyrical participation.

editorial2.imageAlt: Brendan on the DJ Brendan Radio set wearing headphones and gold-frame sunglasses, microphone in front of him
paragraphs: Brendan's growth is amplified by a professional, accessible space built for exactly this. The Remix Studio gives him real gear — a Blue Yeti microphone, a Rode Wireless GO II system, and intuitive audio software — plus a safe place to explore, experiment, and be heard.
It isn't just about recording. It's about building real-world digital media skills, at his pace, around his passion.

pathway.lead: Who's to Say builds possibility paths around an individual's strengths — never a one-size-fits-all job program. For Brendan, that path sings. Here's the Digital Content Creator pathway we're building with him, and for every young adult who walks in next.

cards.title: Music as a voice.
cards.body: Broadway, classic rock, and a microphone. We follow what lights him up and let communication grow through the songs he loves.

cards.title: Real skills, real gear.
cards.body: Spotify navigation, playlist shares, mic technique, and clean transitions — on professional tools, with personalized coaching.

cards.title: On air, and independent.
cards.body: DJ Brendan Radio — a show he hosts and publishes himself. The foundation's vision of independent content creation, made real.

photoband.titleHtml:
His name in lights, his playlist on the system, his voice on the mic.
That's what "unbound potential" looks like.
photoband.imageAlt: DJ Brendan on a concert stage with a giant DJ BRENDAN video wall, mixer, and purple-and-blue lights

donation.titleHtml: Brendan's show doesn't need you to fix anything.
donation.lead: It needs resources. Studio time. Gear that works. A coach across the table who says — yes, cue it up, let's hear it. Your gift makes that table available to more young adults like Brendan.

tiers.name: The Microphone
tiers.blurb: Buys one young adult their first real tool — and the belief that comes with it.
items: A mic, a brush, or a season's pass

tiers.name: The Mentor Chair
tiers.blurb: Funds an entire month of one-on-one creative coaching for one young adult.
items: A real brief, a real deliverable

tiers.name: The Studio
tiers.blurb: Underwrites studio access, pro equipment, and real business development — for a full quarter.
items: Launch plan for one creator's brand

finalCta.lead: Your gift to Who's to Say Foundation funds the studio, the mentor, and the table where dreams like Brendan's get built. Not as a program. As a pathway.

footer.brandBlurb: We empower young adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities to turn their dreams into real, visible work — whatever shape those dreams take.
